title: T-010 — GET /api/scanner/lookup/:type/:barcode unified resolver (06-a)
prompt:
# T-010 — unified barcode lookup endpoint

PRD: 06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.1 FR-SC-BE-011, §10 GS1 parsing, §14.5 SLO
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Provide a single typed lookup endpoint used by every scan input: type ∈ { lp, location, product, po, to, wo, auto }; auto resolves by prefix or GS1 GTIN.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/lookup/[type]/[barcode]/route.ts` (GET).
2. type='auto': run gs1-parser (T-003); if GTIN matched ⇒ products lookup; else regex prefix LP/LOC/PO/TO/WO; else return SC_INVALID_BARCODE.
3. RLS scoped via session org_id + site_id.
4. Response shape: `{ kind: 'lp' | 'location' | 'product' | 'po' | 'to' | 'wo', data: <kind-specific summary> }` or 404 SC_LP_NOT_FOUND etc.
5. SLO target P95 <200ms; ensure single SQL roundtrip per kind.

## Files
- apps/scanner/src/app/api/scanner/lookup/[type]/[barcode]/route.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/src/server/services/scanner-lookup.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/__tests__/api/scanner-lookup.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a barcode '0110012345678902' (GS1 GTIN), when GET /api/scanner/lookup/auto/<barcode> runs, then it resolves kind='product' for the GTIN and the response data carries product code
2. Given a barcode 'LP-OUT-OF-ORG' belonging to org B, when org A user calls GET /lookup/lp/<barcode>, then it returns 404 SC_LP_NOT_FOUND (RLS isolation)
3. Given an unknown prefix 'XYZ123', when GET /lookup/auto/<barcode> runs, then it returns 400 SC_INVALID_BARCODE

## Test strategy
- RED: write scanner-lookup.test.ts asserting all three ACs before implementation
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner test api/scanner-lookup'

## Risk red lines
- Do not return cross-org rows even on cache hit
- Do not loop multiple SQL roundtrips per lookup
- Do not bypass the gs1-parser for auto type

pipeline_inputs.description: Unified GET /api/scanner/lookup endpoint resolving lp/location/product/po/to/wo plus GS1-aware auto type, RLS-scoped.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single Next.js route handler dispatches per :type. auto first delegates to gs1-parser (T-003) to extract GTIN, then falls back to prefix regex. Each kind issues a single RLS-scoped Drizzle query and returns a normalized envelope. SLO target P95 <200ms.
out_of_scope: Do not implement scanner-side cache here
Do not implement audit ingestion endpoint here (T-011)
Do not couple this to warehouse-specific lock APIs (T-034)

Given a barcode 'LP-OUT-OF-ORG' in org B, when org A user calls GET /lookup/lp/<barcode>, then it returns 404 SC_LP_NOT_FOUND (RLS)
Given an unknown prefix 'XYZ123', when GET /lookup/auto/<barcode> runs, then 400 SC_INVALID_BARCODE

Do not loop multiple SQL roundtrips per lookup
